revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大膳職]]
'''Daizen-shiki''' (大膳職) was an {{ill|Executive (government)|lt=administrative office|ja|官司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Executive (government)|1=kanshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452091}} of the {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry (Ritsuryō)|ja|宮内省 (律令制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Ministry (Ritsuryō)|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household (Ritsuryō system)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125558338}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} in Japan. Its primary role was to prepare and provide meals for the courtiers at the {{ill|Imperial Court of Japan|ja|朝廷 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Imperial Court of Japan|qid=Q30253177}}. Before the establishment of the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|qid=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}, the duties were handled by an office known as the {{ill|Zen-shiki|ja|膳職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zen-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With the promulgation of the Taihō Code, the office was divided into the {{ill|Naizen-shi|ja|内膳司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naizen-shi|1=Naizen-shi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11394413}}, responsible for the Emperor's meals, and the Daizen-shiki, which managed the preparation and service of banquet meals. However, staple foods were overseen by the {{ill|Ōiryō|en|Ōiryō|qid=Q8081513|lt=Bureau of Palace Kitchens|1=Ōiryō}}, leaving the Daizen-shiki to handle side dishes, condiments, procurement, manufacturing, cooking, and distribution. According to the {{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2575743}}, it provided items such as {{ill|shi|ja|醢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩辛|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩辛|1=Shiokara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1142503}} (fermented meat or fish), {{ill|醬|en|醬|qid=Q11644487|lt=hishio|1=醬}} (fermented sauces), {{ill|miso|ja|未醤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/miso|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, fruits, and grain-based mochi products. It operated subsidiary organizations such as the "Kagashi-dokoro" (菓餅所) for fruits and mochi and the {{ill|Shōin|ja|醤院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōin|1=Shōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11644496}} for fermented products. Historical documents like the {{ill|East Asian government position Hishō|ja|官職秘抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Asian government position Hishō|1=Kanshoku Hishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452127}}, {{ill|Shokugen-shō|ja|職原鈔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokugen-shō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職原抄|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 職原抄|1=Shokugen-shō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18233321}}, and {{ill|Hyakurō Kunyō-shō|ja|百寮訓要抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakurō Kunyō-shō|1=Hyakurō Kunyō-shō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580725}} indicate that the long-term trends included the chief (''Daibu'') being selected from Ranks above the standard equivalent of {{ill|Shōgoi|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, while deputies (''Suke'') were often chosen from lower Ranks like {{ill|Rokui|ja|六位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位|1=Senior Sixth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545372}}. Additionally, a reform in the {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō era|1=Daidō}} increased the number of ''Shōshin'' and ''Shōzoku'' staff members by one each. == Staff == * '''Daibu''' (大夫): Chief officer ({{ill|Shōgoi|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}, later {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushii|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} equivalent) – 1 person ''' Assistant ''Gon Daibu'' (権大夫) – 1 person * '''Suke''' (亮): Deputy officer ({{ill|Jugo|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} equivalent) – 1 person ''' Assistant ''Gon Suke'' (権亮) – 1 person * '''Taijō''' (大進): Senior administrator ({{ill|Juroku|ja|従六位下|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} equivalent) – 1 person * '''Shōshin''' (少進): Junior administrator ({{ill|Shichii|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}} equivalent) – 1 person * '''Daisakan''' (大属): Senior secretary ({{ill|Hachii|ja|正八位上|1=Senior Eighth Rank|lt=Senior Eighth Rank, U11er Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正八位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正八位}} equivalent) – 1 person * '''Shōsakan''' (少属): Junior secretary ({{ill|Hachii|ja|従八位上|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, U11er Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachii|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}} equivalent) – 1 person * '''Hishio no Tsukasa''' (主醤): Officer of fermented products ({{ill|Shichii|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}} equivalent) – 2 people * '''Kudamono no Tsukasa''' (主果餅): Officer of fruits and mochi ({{ill|Shichii|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}} equivalent) – 2 people * '''Shishō''' (史生): Clerical staff * '''Shokushō''' (職掌): Operational staff * '''Kashiwade-be''' (膳部): Kitchen workers (160 people) * '''Shibu''' (使部): Service workers (30 people) * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁): Supervisors (2 people) * '''Kushibu''' (駈使部): Laborers (80 people) Additionally, there were auxiliary personnel such as fishers, net-makers, and workers associated with miso production. == Other Information == According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} listed three shrines associated with the Daizen-shiki as of the {{ill|Enchō|en|Enchō|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō era|1=Enchō}} (circa 927 CE): the {{ill|Miketsu Shrine|ja|御食津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miketsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Suminomiya Shrine|lt=Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takabe Shrine|ja|高倍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takabe Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (see {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kyōto and its surroundings|ja|宮中・京中の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kyōto and its surroundings|1=List of Shikinaisha in the Imperial Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453224}}). Additionally, excerpts from the {{ill|Takahashi Ujibumi|en|Takahashi Ujibumi|qid=Q1108567|lt=Takahashi-shi Monjo|1=Takahashi Ujibumi}}, as cited in works such as ''Honchō Gekirei,'' associate the {{ill|Miketsu kami|ja|御食津神|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Miketsu-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miketsu kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} with the {{ill|Awa Shrine|en|Awa Shrine|qid=Q705547|lt=Awa Shrine|1=Awa Shrine}} in modern-day {{ill|Tateyama, Chiba|en|Tateyama, Chiba|qid=Q646755|lt=Tateyama, Chiba|1=Tateyama, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture. == Notes == {{Notes-help|qq=}} {{reflist|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁管理部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|1=Maintenance and Works Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453311}} – Modern equivalent: ''Daizen Division.''
{{translated page|ja|大膳職|version=102917651|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:大膳職]]
<!--Dazai Gon no Daibu--><!--Dazai Gon no Daibu--><!--Dazai Gon no Daibu--><!--Dazai Gon no Daibu--><!--Dazai Gon no Daibu--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11438575}} [[Category:History of food culture in Japan]] [[Category:Officials of the Ritsuryō System]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
